distribution.summary: Split the conflated scaffold into three repos with hard boundaries: (1) a PUBLIC PRODUCT repo — the OSS Knowledge Plane, GitHub-canonical, Apache-2.0 (recommend MIT OR Apache-2.0 dual for Curio symmetry), Python 3.12+/uv workspace monorepo, re-scaffolded fresh under a new name (Alex's call) rather than surgically cleaned — containing all packages, published JSON Schema contracts, a profile-based docker-compose reference deployment, docs with a CI-enforced <30-min quickstart, and the full tight-DX layer; (2) Alex's PRIVATE INSTANCE repo (repurpose git-forge alex/knowledge-plane) holding kp.toml with real endpoints (llm-gateway/managed-postgres/G11), ADR-0029 placement as compose/Ansible overrides, and secret references (values in secrets-manager/Keychain only); (3) the PRIVATE canonical VAULT repo, where now.md moves. Native Curio integration is enforced by construction: the KP accepts vanilla frontmatter-v1/events-v1 via a kp-ingest adapter (never a custom Curio template), vendors Curio's pinned schemas, runs cross-repo contract tests in CI, never writes into Curio-owned notes (lifecycle state lives index-side keyed on curio_id; enrichment via companion notes), and files the events-v1 schema review (negation events, tags-in-payload, ts/cursor spec) as consumer #1 now. The architectural keystone for distributability is the index-service seam: kp-mcp is a stateless MCP adapter (per the 2026-07-28 RC) over kp-index's query API — in-process on one box, HTTP in split deployments — which makes vector/graph store choice pure config (embedded sqlite-vec+FTS5+Kuzu default; Postgres/pgvector profile) and dissolves the cross-host argument that forced the pgvector lean. ADR-0029 survives untouched (its placements become instance configuration); RFC-0018 needs nine explicit amendments, listed. Derived indices never migrate — they rebuild from a self-describing index-meta.json epoch, which is the whole upgrade story.
distribution.layout:
REPO 1 — PRODUCT (public, GitHub-canonical; name TBD by Alex, placeholder <kp>; fresh scaffold, not surgery on b5a8c36):
<kp>/
├── pyproject.toml            # uv workspace root, Python 3.12+ — replaces package.json; Node dropped entirely
├── justfile                  # setup · demo · up/down · ingest · reindex · mcp · librarian-once · digest-once · test · lint · typecheck · schema-gen · schema-check · config-check · release
├── kp.example.toml           # THE one config file a stranger edits (schema: contracts/kp-config.v1.json)
├── .env.example              # KP_* env overrides + Paperless-style _FILE docker-secrets convention
├── LICENSE / GOVERNANCE.md / SECURITY.md   # Apache-2.0 (or MIT OR Apache-2.0); DCO not CLA; personal-first governance (Curio precedent)
├── packages/
│   ├── kp-core/              # (was shared) pydantic-v2 models → published JSON Schemas; config loader/validator; vault I/O: atomic tmp+rename writes, frontmatter parse, checksum, .curio/manifest.json awareness (write-ownership guard), identity resolution (curio_id | zotero_key | checksum)
│   ├── kp-ingest/            # (was ingest, re-scoped) Zotero puller, web-clip, transcript driver (whisper.cpp, batch), CURIO ADAPTER (frontmatter-v1 reader + events-v1 cursor consumer, rotation-aware; webhook receiver), minimal feature-flagged RSS fallback for non-Curio users
│   ├── kp-index/             # (was index) OCR router (Docling MIT default; AGPL/GPL engines as optional subprocess-isolated extras), chunker, embedding client (OpenAI-compatible HTTP), store DRIVERS: embedded (sqlite-vec + FTS5 + Kuzu files) | postgres (pgvector + tsvector + edge tables); checksum-keyed incremental watcher; INDEX QUERY SERVICE — internal API kp-mcp calls (in-process single-box, HTTP split)
│   ├── kp-mcp/               # (was mcp-retrieval; absorbs vault MCP — built in-repo, thin headless file surface) stateless FastMCP servers per MCP 2026-07-28 RC: search_hybrid, find_contradicting, unlinked_neighbors + vault list/read/search/append/patch tools; stdio default, Streamable HTTP + bearer optional
│   ├── kp-zotero/            # NEW — the differentiator: zotero.org Web API metadata cache (Last-Modified-Version polling) + WebDAV .prop/.zip fulltext extraction shim → derived attachment cache + Zotero MCP surface
│   └── kp-librarian/         # prompts/ as plain markdown (harness-agnostic), skills/, scheduler (cron/systemd), SAFE-OUTPUTS VALIDATOR (deterministic schema+link+checksum gates on proposals/* — agent proposes, separately-privileged process disposes), harness adapters: claude-code (reference) + openai-loop (provider-neutral)
├── contracts/                # kp-note.v1.json · kp-config.v1.json · digest-feed.v1.md (Atom convention, stable GUIDs) · ranking-hints.v1 (reserved, named only) · vendored/ pinned Curio frontmatter-v1 + events-v1 · per-schema CHANGELOGs
├── deploy/
│   ├── compose.yml           # COMPOSE_PROFILES: core (default, single container, embedded stores) | ollama | postgres | zotero | librarian | curio-webhook — CPU-only images, no CUDA anywhere
│   ├── compose.split.yml     # two-box reference: mcp tier ↔ index tier over the query API
│   └── hypervisor/              # Alex's ADR-0029 shape documented as ONE profile, genericized (no LAN IPs)
├── docs/                     # quickstart.md (<30 min, CI-executed) · concepts/canonical-derived.md · integrations/{curio,zotero,obsidian,generic-reader}.md · operations/{backup,rebuild,upgrade}.md · reference/{config,cli,mcp-tools}.md
├── examples/sample-vault/    # seeded demo vault + fixtures — `just demo` = clone → uv sync → ingest→index→search_hybrid on one machine
├── .claude/                  # CLAUDE.md (canonical/derived law, contract list, proposals-branch-only doctrine, repo rituals) + skills: /propose /digest-once /reindex /schema-bump /contract-check /release
├── .github/workflows/        # ci.yml: lint/type/test matrix (3.12/3.13, linux+macos) + demo-smoke (quickstart headless) + cross-repo contract job (pull Curio schema artifacts, validate fixtures both ways) + compose-profile builds + gitleaks; release.yml: tag → PyPI + ghcr CPU multi-arch images + schema artifacts + git-cliff changelog
└── .githooks/                # ruff+format, mypy, conventional-commit msg, schema-regen drift gate, gitleaks

REPO 2 — INSTANCE (private, git-forge-canonical; repurpose alex/knowledge-plane → alex/kp-instance, never mirrored out):
├── kp.toml                   # real values: llm.base_url=llm-gateway 192.0.2.5, embedding=Ollama-on-pve, index.store=postgres (managed-postgres supavisor DSN, pending pgvector confirm), mcp.transport=http on G11, librarian.notify=push-notifier (LAN pull, ADR-0028), curio.events_dir=vault .curio/events (in-vault, his private choice)
├── secrets/                  # env-file TEMPLATES + secrets-manager/Keychain pointers per secrets-registry doctrine — values never committed anywhere
├── deploy/                   # compose overrides + the management plane's ansible wiring for ADR-0029: CT 206 (zotero WebDAV+kp-zotero), kp-mcp CT + vault git-mirror unit on G11, index+librarian VM on pve (claude-agent pattern), caddy ingress, digest feed served from G11
├── PIN                       # product repo pinned at release tag/SHA (submodule or image-tag file); bump = deliberate PR ritual
└── notes/                    # instance runbook: mirror sync, curio webhook shim, llm-observability project wiring (observability rides llm-gateway's callback — product repo knows nothing of llm-observability)

REPO 3 — VAULT (private, canonical; new repo, Alex's manual creation):
├── inbox/ (curio/ subdir = Curio named destination) · proposals/ (librarian branches + digest-YYYY-MM-DD.md) · notes/zettels/evergreens
├── now.md                    # MOVES HERE from the monorepo — canonical hand-authored steering, path configurable ([vault].now_path)
└── .curio/manifest.json (+ events/ in Alex's instance)  # Curio-owned, committed — required for wipe-and-reinstall reconciliation

Estate wiring: product repo → RFC-0016 Mode A reference pull-mirror into git-forge mirrors/ (read-only estate visibility); instance + vault repos git-forge-canonical; management plane consumes the instance repo per RFC-0014/ADR-0022 (pinned submodule under ansible/roles/**/files/ only where code runs as a managed guest).

key_decisions.decision: Three-repo split: public product repo (GitHub-canonical) / private instance repo (git-forge, repurposed alex/knowledge-plane) / private canonical vault repo; vault/ and now.md leave the monorepo
key_decisions.rationale: The scaffold conflates distributable machinery with Alex's canonical personal content and LAN endpoints — canonical/derived is also a repo-lifecycle split (different backup, visibility, and mutation postures); distributability is impossible while instance data sits in the system repo

key_decisions.decision: Fresh public re-scaffold under a new name instead of cleaning b5a8c36
key_decisions.rationale: One throwaway commit exists, no secrets in history but LAN IPs + Alex-narration + three pre-ADR-0029-amendment falsehoods (.mcp.json, compose.yml, README) throughout — a clean start is cheaper than surgery and yields a public-safe history by construction

key_decisions.decision: Single-language Python 3.12+/uv workspace; drop Node (package.json, '@plane/*' naming); no Rust in the KP
key_decisions.rationale: Every OCR tool RFC-0018 names is Python, FastMCP is the mature MCP SDK, all store clients are first-class Python, embeddings arrive over HTTP (no in-process ML); Curio owns the Rust side and the seam is file contracts — Node would force strangers to install two runtimes for zero benefit; cost is ~zero now (packages are empty READMEs)

key_decisions.decision: Index-service seam: kp-mcp is a stateless MCP adapter over kp-index's query API — in-process on one box, HTTP in split deployments
key_decisions.rationale: Makes store choice orthogonal to placement: solves the graph-store cross-host problem ADR-0029 flagged only for vectors (embedded Kuzu was unreachable from G11 by the same argument), demotes pgvector from architectural necessity to instance option, and aligns with the MCP 2026-07-28 RC's stateless-server direction

key_decisions.decision: Embedded-by-default stores (sqlite-vec + SQLite FTS5 + Kuzu) with a Postgres profile (pgvector + tsvector + edge tables) behind one driver interface; the system owns BM25/FTS
key_decisions.rationale: A zero-infra single-container core avoids the RAGFlow heavyweight trap and keeps the 30-min quickstart honest; FTS must be system-owned because the 'free via Omnisearch' plan died with the live-Obsidian-app dependency and Obsidian may never be a hard dependency

key_decisions.decision: Curio integration by adapter, never by template: KP accepts VANILLA frontmatter-v1/events-v1, vendors pinned schemas, maps fields in kp-ingest; lifecycle status lives index-side keyed on curio_id; KP never writes into manifest-listed notes; enrichment ships as companion notes first, managed-region in-note edits post-v1
key_decisions.rationale: A custom Curio template on Alex's instance would break frontmatter-v1 validation and make his install non-representative of the OSS story; Curio re-promotion re-renders whole files, silently clobbering any injected status field or in-body edit

key_decisions.decision: kp-note.v1 reconciles with Curio at the mandatory schema-review gate: adopt schema_version, bare sha256-of-normalized-body checksum (drop the 'sha256:' prefix), source = URL (Curio semantics), origin enum renamed to `kind` (zotero|web|manual|transcript|rss) for OCR routing, status removed from frontmatter — executed as the deliberate migration the KP schema doc itself mandates
key_decisions.rationale: The 'locked' schema conflicts field-by-field with consumer-#1's published contract; a wrong identity key frozen now is a breaking change on both sides — the KP files the review findings (negation events, tags in event payload, ts/cursor spec) immediately, months before Curio Phase 3 freezes

key_decisions.decision: Digest = digest note in vault proposals/ (primary, git-native) + optional Atom digest feed with stable GUIDs (Curio-subscribable, pending its private-network allowlist W1) + pluggable notifier (none | push-notifier LAN-pull | webhook | stdout)
key_decisions.rationale: ADR-0028 governs: push-notifier is LAN-only pull with no background push, cloud push is forbidden; the durable artifact must be the note itself, and the feed closes the discovery loop inside the reading surface

key_decisions.decision: Librarian ships as harness-agnostic prompts/skills + scheduler + a deterministic safe-outputs validator (schema/link/checksum CI gates on proposals/*, merge rights withheld from librarian credentials); Claude Code adapter is the reference, an OpenAI-compatible loop is the second v1 adapter
key_decisions.rationale: The OSS system must not require a Claude subscription (mirrors the Obsidian recommended-not-required posture); 'agent proposes, a separately-privileged process disposes' is the 2026-proven security model and the house already owns the CI-gate idiom

key_decisions.decision: kp-zotero package: two-channel design — zotero.org Web API for metadata (cached, rate-limit polite) + a WebDAV .prop/.zip extraction shim for fulltext, co-located with the WebDAV store
key_decisions.rationale: The accepted 'MCP reads a WebDAV-synced copy of the library' data path does not exist — WebDAV carries only attachments and the web API cannot serve WebDAV-stored files; only the WebDAV host can serve those bytes, which strengthens CT-206 colocation and is a genuine market differentiator (nobody serves this niche)

key_decisions.decision: OCR default is Docling (MIT); PyMuPDF4LLM (AGPL) and Marker (GPL-class) are optional user-installed extras invoked via subprocess isolation, never in published images
key_decisions.rationale: Copyleft engines in an Apache-licensed distribution are the one real licensing landmine; subprocess isolation + user installation keeps the default distribution clean while preserving the RFC's routing cascade for those who opt in

key_decisions.decision: License Apache-2.0 (recommend MIT OR Apache-2.0 dual matching Curio); DCO not CLA; naming is Alex's decision and blocks publication ('knowledge-plane' is generic and collides with the Clark-et-al networking term; run Curio's trademark/domain clearance ritual)
key_decisions.rationale: Khoj's AGPL is documented adoption friction and Curio's own analysis rejected copyleft for local-first tools — the open contracts are the lock-in defense; sibling-project symmetry lowers contributor friction

key_decisions.decision: Derived indices never migrate: index dirs carry index-meta.json {kp_version, contract versions, embedding model+dims, schema_epoch}; on mismatch the server refuses to serve and offers `kp reindex --rebuild`; embedding-model swap = deliberate epoch bump; canonical-side frontmatter majors ship a `kp migrate` codemod that writes via a proposal branch, never directly
key_decisions.rationale: Disposability is the accepted design's superpower — exploiting it eliminates the entire migration-script maintenance class for derived data while keeping the index self-describing against silent embedding drift

key_decisions.decision: Release engineering: SemVer, Conventional Commits + squash merge, git-cliff changelog; each tag publishes PyPI packages, ghcr CPU-only multi-arch images, and versioned JSON Schema artifacts with stable $id URLs and per-schema CHANGELOGs
key_decisions.rationale: Mirrors both the house ritual and Curio's schemars pattern; stable schema $ids give downstream consumers (including Curio's own tests) a pinning target — the W3 wish-list item applied to the KP's own contracts

key_decisions.decision: Distributability is CI-enforced, not aspirational: a demo-smoke job executes the quickstart headlessly against examples/sample-vault, and a cross-repo contract job pulls Curio's published schema artifacts and validates KP parser fixtures every run
key_decisions.rationale: House doctrine — deterministic gates over 'remember to check'; the <30-min promise and the 'native, not bolt-on' claim both rot silently without machinery

key_decisions.decision: Events default: OSS keeps events-v1 JSONL outside the vault repo (XDG state dir) with in-vault as a documented option; Alex's instance points it at the vault's .curio/events/
key_decisions.rationale: Behavioral reading history committed to git is a privacy foot-gun for strangers; Alex's vault is private so his in-vault choice is instance config, and .curio/manifest.json stays committed regardless (the reconcile story requires it)

distribution.contracts: PUBLISHED by the KP (all pydantic-v2 → JSON Schema artifacts per release, stable $id URLs, per-schema CHANGELOG, additive-in-minor/breaking-in-major, mirroring Curio's schemars pattern): (1) kp-note.v1 — frontmatter for KP-authored notes (Zotero literature notes, web clips, transcripts): title, kind (zotero|web|manual|transcript|rss — OCR routing), source (URL), zotero_key, doi, authors, published, saved, tags, checksum (bare sha256 of normalized body, Curio-aligned), schema_version; lifecycle status deliberately absent (index-side, keyed on identity: curio_id | zotero_key | checksum precedence). (2) kp-config.v1 — the kp.toml schema, validated by `kp config check`; sections [vault]{path, git_remote?, proposals_branch_prefix, now_path} [curio]{enabled, inbox_dir, events=file|webhook|both|none, events_dir, cursor_state, webhook_bind} [zotero]{enabled, user_id, api_key_env, webdav_url, webdav_user_env/password_env, attachment_cache} [llm]{base_url (default http://localhost:11434/v1), api_key_env, model} [embedding]{base_url, model, chunking} [index]{store=embedded|postgres, postgres_dsn_env, data_dir} [ocr]{engine=docling|pymupdf4llm|marker, max_concurrency} [mcp]{transport=stdio|http, http_bind, bearer_token_env} [librarian]{harness=claude-code|openai-loop|none, maintenance_cron, digest_cron, digest_feed, notify=none|push-notifier|webhook|stdout, budget caps}; secrets only via *_env / _FILE indirection, never values in the file. (3) digest-feed.v1 — Atom convention: stable per-digest GUIDs, digest note in proposals/ is source of truth, feed is a projection (formalizes Curio wish-list W6 so ANY conforming reader subscribes). (4) MCP tool surface — search_hybrid, find_contradicting, unlinked_neighbors + vault file tools (list/read/search/append/patch-by-heading), versioned tool schemas, stateless per MCP spec 2026-07-28 RC. (5) index query API (internal but versioned) — the kp-mcp↔kp-index seam; in-process or HTTP, same shape. (6) ranking-hints.v1 — RESERVED name only, the future Curio-triage-ranking seam (R5). CONSUMED by the KP (pinned copies vendored in contracts/vendored/, validated in CI, tolerate additive minors / hard-fail majors): Curio frontmatter-v1 + events-v1 (the KP is designated consumer #1 and owes the schema review NOW: negation events, tags in article payload, ts format/ordering + cursor opacity — filed as Curio issues before its Phase 3 freeze); Zotero Web API v3 (metadata; Last-Modified-Version etiquette); the de-facto WebDAV .prop/.zip attachment format (officially unsupported upstream — pinned fixture zips in tests as the tripwire); any OpenAI-compatible /v1 endpoint for LLM + embeddings (llm-gateway and Ollama both just work — this is the pluggability seam that makes llm-gateway an instance choice, not a dependency). Versioning story: KP schema majors are breaking releases with a written policy; derived-index compatibility is epoch-stamped (index-meta.json), never migrated — rebuild is the upgrade path.
distribution.alex_instance_vs_oss: Generic system (product repo): any markdown+YAML directory as vault (Obsidian recommended viewer, never a dependency — guaranteed architecturally by the headless git-mirror/file-based MCP design); embedded stores (sqlite-vec+FTS5+Kuzu) in one container; Ollama-on-localhost defaults for LLM+embeddings via the OpenAI-compatible seam; stdio MCP for single-user .mcp.json wiring; notifier=none; librarian optional with either harness adapter; events JSONL in XDG state; deployed by compose profiles on any single box; GitHub CI; no git-forge, llm-gateway, llm-observability, managed-postgres, push-notifier, or hypervisor anywhere in code or defaults. Alex's instance (instance repo overrides, config-only — zero forked code, which IS the acceptance test that his install stays representative): llm.base_url → llm-gateway CT 106 (virtual keys; llm-observability observability rides llm-gateway's callback — the product repo has no llm-observability concept); embedding → Ollama on pve; index.store=postgres → managed-postgres CT 111 via Supavisor (pending the one-line pgvector confirmation; embedded-on-pve is his fallback); mcp.transport=http behind caddy ingress on G11 per ADR-0029 (kp-mcp CT + vault git-mirror unit on G11; kp-zotero co-located with the WebDAV store on CT 206 = 192.0.2.15; index+librarian VM on pve cloning the claude-agent pattern with RFC-0013 workspaces for proposals/); notify=push-notifier CT 203 (LAN-pull per ADR-0028); digest Atom feed served from G11; curio.events_dir → in-vault .curio/events/; Curio desktop on the Mac writes into the local vault clone (Mac canonical, G11 mirror — the management-plane doctrine applied); vault git_remote → git-forge; now.md in his vault repo. Secrets: Mac Keychain canonical + secrets-manager escrow, referenced via env files Ansible provisions — never in any repo. Repo/mirroring topology: product repo GitHub-canonical with an RFC-0016 Mode A reference pull-mirror into git-forge mirrors/ (read-only estate visibility; needs a small RFC-0016 note since neither mode contemplates community-canonical dev — recommend Alex develops the product Mac-side against GitHub); instance + vault repos git-forge-canonical, never mirrored out; the management plane consumes the instance repo per RFC-0014/ADR-0022 pinned-submodule rules; the instance pins the product at a release tag and bumps via deliberate PR.
risks: Curio schema-review timing: events-v1 as published is monotone-only (no unstar/unread/read_later_removed), lacks tags in the article payload, and leaves ts/cursor semantics unspecified — if the KP (consumer #1 and designated reviewer) doesn't file the review before Curio Phase 3 freezes (~11-14.5 weeks out), a joint breaking change gets baked into both systems' first contract.

The WebDAV .prop/.zip format is officially unsupported by Zotero ('read the source... we don't provide support') — kp-zotero's fulltext shim can break on any Zotero release; mitigation is pinned fixture archives in the contract test suite as a tripwire, but upstream breakage remains a standing maintenance tax on the system's differentiator.

Copyleft contamination: PyMuPDF4LLM is AGPL and Marker is GPL-class with shifting commercial terms — any packaging slip that bundles them into published images or imports them in-process compromises the Apache license; terms must be re-verified at release time, and the subprocess-isolation boundary needs a CI gate, not convention.

pgvector in the pinned managed-postgres PG15 image is still unconfirmed and the PG15→17 migration is one-way and undone — Alex's postgres-profile choice could stall at Phase 3; the embedded default de-risks the OSS story but not his instance (fallback: embedded stores on the pve VM behind the index-service seam, which the architecture now permits).

pve real utilization is unmeasured while nominal allocation already exceeds physical RAM — CPU-only embedding + OCR + librarian on the M5 Ultra may force ADR-0029's named fallback (scaled-down cadence/scope), which degrades the discovery loop's usefulness before it proves itself.

Naming is a double blocker: 'knowledge-plane' is unbrandable and collides with an established networking-research term, and Curio's own trademark clearance (Zengobi) is unresolved — public integration docs can't even name the reader; both decisions are Alex's and gate publication, not development.

Community-canonical GitHub dev inverts the house git-forge-centric flow and RFC-0016's modes don't cover it (Mode B's push-mirror never pulls external merges) — without a small process note, the estate mirror drifts or someone commits git-forge-side to a repo whose truth lives on GitHub.

Librarian harness neutrality is the most likely under-investment: if only the Claude Code adapter is real at v1, the OSS discovery loop requires a Claude subscription — contradicting the pluggability requirement the same way an Obsidian dependency would; the OpenAI-compatible loop needs genuine v1 budget and its own CI exercise.

Instance/product drift: Alex's install is the reference deployment — if the instance pin lags releases or accumulates config the product schema doesn't express, his install stops being representative and the OSS story silently forks; the pin-bump ritual and 'zero forked code' rule need the same gate treatment as everything else.

MCP spec churn: the 2026-07-28 RC (stateless servers, Mcp-Method routing) finalizes mid-build — building against the RC risks rework if it slips, building against 2025-06-18 stable ships immediately-legacy servers; decision: target the RC, pin the SDK, isolate transport in kp-mcp so a spec delta is one package's problem.

Quickstart rot: the <30-minute promise is the distributability acceptance test and dies silently without the CI demo-smoke job staying green on both Linux and macOS runners — treat a red demo job as a release blocker, not a flaky test.

Behavioral privacy in the OSS default: events-v1 is reading history — if docs or defaults ever steer strangers to commit it into a shared/synced vault repo, the project inherits a privacy incident; the XDG-state default plus an explicit docs warning is the guard, and Alex's in-vault choice must stay clearly labeled instance-private.
rfc0018_amendments: §Index — BM25/full-text: delete 'free via the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch'. That implementation requires the live desktop app ADR-0029's amendment dropped, and violates the Obsidian-never-a-dependency distribution rule. The system owns FTS: SQLite FTS5 in the embedded default, Postgres tsvector in the postgres profile, both behind the index driver interface.

§Ingestion + §Rollout Phase 2 — RSS re-scope: Curio (frontmatter-v1 export + events-v1 stream) is the primary RSS/reading path; the KP-built RSS fetcher is demoted to a minimal, feature-flagged fallback for non-Curio users; the ingestion plane's first-class scope becomes Zotero PDFs, web clips, and transcripts. Two parallel RSS pipelines with divergent normalization was the failure mode.

§Placement table + §Access — Zotero data path corrected: 'Zotero MCP reads a WebDAV-synced local copy of the library' describes a path that doesn't exist (WebDAV carries only attachments as unsupported .prop/.zip pairs; metadata rides zotero.org; the web API cannot serve WebDAV-stored files). Amended to the two-channel design: Web API metadata (cached) + a local WebDAV zip-extraction shim co-located on CT 206. Placement unchanged — colocation is strengthened.

§Access + §Alternatives — MCP sourcing corrected: 'reads zotero.sqlite directly' and the named candidate list are stale (sqlite never reaches G11; coddingtonbear/cyanheads require the live app). The vault MCP is built in-repo (thin headless file surface — the distributable answer); the Zotero MCP is the in-repo kp-zotero package (or, if 54yyyu/zotero-mcp is wrapped, its ChromaDB semantic search is disabled to preserve single-derived-index discipline). 'Only retrieval-fusion is custom' is retracted.

§Placement table — 'push-notifier digest delivery' respecified under ADR-0028: the primary artifact is the digest note in vault proposals/ (git-native, durable), optionally projected as an Atom feed the reading surface subscribes to; the notifier is pluggable (none | push-notifier LAN-pull | webhook); no cloud push path exists or may be added.

§Open Questions — vector store question reframed and extended to the graph store: the cross-host argument that forced the pgvector lean dissolves because kp-index exposes a query API that kp-mcp calls in split deployments (in-process on one box). Store choice becomes configuration: embedded sqlite-vec+FTS5+Kuzu default, Postgres/pgvector profile; Alex's instance may still choose managed-postgres pgvector — as instance config, not architecture. The graph store (Kùzu, embedded) gets the same resolution the RFC previously gave only vectors.

NEW §Distribution (the structural scope change): the KP is an open-source product plus a private instance — three-repo split (public product / private instance config / private canonical vault), GitHub-canonical product under a permissive license (Apache-2.0 or MIT OR Apache-2.0), pluggable seams (OpenAI-compatible LLM+embeddings, store drivers, notifier, agent harness, git remote), Obsidian recommended viewer never dependency, llm-gateway/llm-observability/managed-postgres/git-forge/push-notifier demoted to documented instance choices. Runtime corrected: '@plane/*' Node naming replaced by a Python 3.12+/uv workspace.

§Design spine — 'frontmatter schema locked' unfrozen and reconciled as kp-note.v1 at Curio's mandatory schema-review gate: adopt schema_version, bare sha256-of-normalized-body checksum, source-as-URL with the origin enum renamed to `kind`, lifecycle status moved index-side keyed on curio_id/zotero_key (Curio-owned notes are never edited by the KP). Executed as the deliberate migration the schema doc itself requires.

§Repo boundary — instance data leaves the monorepo: vault/ and now.md move out (now.md becomes an in-vault convention at a configurable path; the vault is its own private repo); the existing git-forge alex/knowledge-plane is repurposed as the instance repo. ADR-0029 is explicitly NOT amended — under the split, every placement it decides becomes instance-side configuration, which validates it; RFC-0016 needs only a short note covering community-canonical (GitHub-first) repos with a Mode A reference mirror for estate visibility.

integration_fabric.summary: The fabric is contract-first: one Curio-anchored note-schema family (kp-note/v1) serves every producer; all lifecycle/agent state moves out of frontmatter into derived stores; every server-side reader consumes the canonical vault through a read-only git mirror on G11; and every agent write enters through proposals/* branches gated by deterministic CI with merge rights withheld from agent credentials (safe-outputs separation). ZOTERO is a two-channel design that fixes RFC-0018's nonexistent data path: metadata flows from zotero.org Web API v3 via Last-Modified-Version delta polling into a disposable local cache on CT 206; attachments flow through the self-hosted nginx WebDAV store on the same CT, where an extraction shim unzips KEY.prop/KEY.zip pairs into a derived fulltext cache keyed on the .prop hash — the KP builds its own thin kp-zotero-mcp (web-API-native + WebDAV-aware, FastMCP) because no existing server serves this niche and 54yyyu's server is desktop-bound with a rogue second embedding store. Literature notes are generated per Better-BibTeX citekey (pinned via Zotero's Extra field so it survives to the web API) with zotero_key as the machine handle and Zotero-canonical annotations mirrored read-only into a managed region. CURIO is native by construction: the KP vendors pinned frontmatter-v1/events-v1 JSON Schemas, validates at ingest (additive-minor tolerated, major quarantined), keys everything on curio_id, treats every .curio/manifest.json-listed path as Curio-owned and never writes it — enrichment ships day 1 as companion notes on proposal branches, with a managed-region marker spec reserved now and filed into Curio's mandatory schema-review gate alongside the missing negation events, event_id ULID, tags-in-payload, and the W1 private-network allowlist that unblocks the bidirectional loop: the librarian's digest is a vault note first (proposal-merged, durable, git-native per ADR-0028) and an Atom feed second (rendered on G11 with stable tag: GUIDs) that Curio subscribes to, closing digest→read/star/promote→events→interest-model inside the reader. INDEX plane resolves pgvector-vs-LanceDB as a driver interface: the OSS default a stranger gets is a single SQLite file (sqlite-vec + FTS5 + relational claim-edge tables — vectors, BM25, and graph in one zero-infra store), with a postgres driver (pgvector + tsvector + the same edge tables) for split deployments; Alex's instance selects postgres against managed-postgres, which solves ADR-0029's cross-host problem for all three retrieval legs at once and drops both Kùzu and LanceDB. Reindex is git-commit-driven (mirror update → name-status diff → checksum-gated re-embed) with a file-watcher mode for all-in-one installs and a periodic checksum sweep as safety net. The system splits into a GitHub-canonical Apache-licensed product repo (Python uv workspace; contracts/ is the product), Alex's private git-forge instance repo (endpoints, compose overrides, fleet placement), and the private canonical vault repo (which absorbs now.md). ADR-0029 survives untouched — its placements become instance configuration — while RFC-0018 needs eight explicit amendments, led by system-owned FTS, the two-channel Zotero path, RSS delegation to Curio, and the digest respecification.
