FLEET (Alex's instance; pure ADR-0029 configuration)
├── G11/pve-net (always-on, I/O-bound only): CT 206 "zotero" = nginx WebDAV (dav_ext, basic-auth) + kp-zotero-mcp + metadata cache + attachment shim; NEW CT 207 "kp-access" = kp-sync mirror (read-only) + kp-vault-mcp + kp-retrieval-mcp + digest-feed renderer + curio-events webhook receiver, behind existing caddy ingress (kp.example.internal) — G11 headroom re-derivation required post-router-fw-cutover before CT 207
└── pve (M5 Ultra, rebooted freely): kp-worker VM/CT (claude-agent pattern) = OCR batch, chunk/embed via llm-gateway, indexer, librarian loops, RFC-0013 NFS worktree for proposals/; index lives in managed-postgres Postgres (pgvector presence: verify one-liner before Phase 3)

key_decisions.decision: One schema family, Curio-anchored: kp-note/v1 adopts frontmatter-v1's core verbatim (source = URL, author string, published/saved, bare-hex checksum of normalized body, schema_version) and layers KP extensions as additional fields (origin enum zotero|web|feed|manual, zotero_key, citekey, doi, authors array, kp_id); the scaffold's 'locked' schema is superseded, treated as the migration its own doc demands
key_decisions.rationale: The OSS KP must accept VANILLA Curio output — a custom template on Alex's install would break frontmatter-v1 validation and make his instance non-representative; Curio's schema is the one with a published breaking-change policy and a review gate, so the KP bends, not Curio. The adapter maps author→authors[0]; origin is derived at ingest for Curio notes (manifest membership) and stamped only in KP-owned notes, so OCR routing survives without touching Curio files

key_decisions.decision: Lifecycle state (unprocessed→linked→promoted) moves index-side for ALL notes, keyed on note_id; the status field is removed from frontmatter entirely
key_decisions.rationale: Curio re-promotion re-renders the whole file from template — any KP-stamped status is silently clobbered. A uniform rule (no Curio special case) keeps frontmatter provenance-only, makes every note's lifecycle queryable in one place, and the state table is derived/rebuildable (re-derive from index membership + graph links + promotion events), honoring the sacred split

key_decisions.decision: Identity is producer-namespaced: note_id = curio:<uuid7> | zotero:<itemKey> | kp:<uuid7>; the index keys on note_id, never on vault path
key_decisions.rationale: Renames are manifest-tracked for Curio and git-tracked for the rest; path-keying breaks on both. Namespacing prevents cross-producer collisions and gives every contract (state table, graph nodes, citations, events) one join key

key_decisions.decision: Manifest-listed = Curio-owned = KP never writes: enrichment ships day 1 as companion notes (notes/re/<slug>.md wikilinking the Curio note) on proposal branches; in-note enrichment waits on the managed-region contract, whose marker syntax the KP reserves NOW in contracts/managed-region/v1.md and files into Curio's schema review
key_decisions.rationale: Dossier R3 verdict: companion notes need zero Curio changes and survive re-promotion clobbering; reserving the marker early keeps existing notes forward-compatible when Curio scopes re-export + checksum to the region post-v1

key_decisions.decision: Zotero is a two-channel fabric on CT 206: (A) metadata via zotero.org Web API v3 with Last-Modified-Version delta polling into a disposable local SQLite cache; (B) attachments via the co-located nginx WebDAV store, with an extraction shim that unzips KEY.prop/KEY.zip pairs into a derived fulltext cache keyed on item key + .prop hash, re-extracting when the hash changes
key_decisions.rationale: The ecosystem scan proved RFC-0018's written path doesn't exist: WebDAV carries only attachments (undocumented .prop/.zip), metadata never leaves zotero.org sync, and the web API cannot serve WebDAV-stored files — only the WebDAV host can, which strengthens the CT 206 colocation. The cache is derived/disposable; the vendored .prop/.zip parser gets contract tests as a format-drift tripwire

key_decisions.decision: Build kp-zotero-mcp in-repo (Python FastMCP, web-API-native + WebDAV-cache-aware) rather than adopting 54yyyu/zotero-mcp or wrapping kujenga's server; document 54yyyu as a Mac-side desktop option only
key_decisions.rationale: 54yyyu's primary read path is the desktop local API (useless on a headless CT) and its built-in ChromaDB semantic search would create a second, divergent embedding store violating single-derived-index discipline; kujenga lacks the WebDAV fulltext path entirely. Nobody serves headless-WebDAV-fulltext — it becomes the OSS differentiator, and in-repo means contract control

key_decisions.decision: Citekeys: Better BibTeX pinned keys, persisted in Zotero's Extra field ('Citation Key: …') so they survive to the web API; deterministic fallback generator (auth-lower + year + first-word) when absent; citekey is the literature-note filename and wikilink handle, zotero_key stays the machine round-trip handle
key_decisions.rationale: BBT keys live in an attached sqlite that never reaches the server — the Extra-field convention is the only headless-visible citekey channel; two handles separate human addressing (stable, readable) from machine addressing (Zotero's own key)

key_decisions.decision: Annotations are Zotero-canonical, mirrored read-only into a managed region of the literature note (kp:begin owner=kp-zotero section=annotations); promoting an annotation copies its text into the human-owned body while the mirror persists
key_decisions.rationale: Resolves brief risk #2: one writer per region kills the round-trip-fidelity problem; the mirror is regenerable (derived) while the note body stays human/evergreen — the same ownership-marker mechanism later serves Curio highlights

key_decisions.decision: Vault mirror: kp-sync on G11 runs a bare repo + worktree updated by a git-forge push-webhook (cron pull fallback), strictly read-only (read-only deploy key, no push credential on G11); every update emits vault.updated with the commit range; the librarian on pve pushes only to proposals/* via a token whose git-forge branch protection forbids main; humans merge
key_decisions.rationale: Extends the management plane's 'server copy is a convenience mirror, Mac canonical' doctrine; the GitHub-Agentic-Workflows safe-outputs separation (agent proposes, separately-privileged deterministic CI disposes) becomes enforceable machinery: the vault repo's gate workflow validates schema/paths/links/Curio-owned-path-untouched before any human sees the diff

key_decisions.decision: Incremental reindex is git-commit-driven: vault.updated → git diff --name-status old..new → per-note checksum comparison → re-embed only changed chunks (chunk-level hashes), update FTS row, re-extract links/claims; all-in-one deployments use a debounced file-watcher (tmp+rename-aware, .curio/ temp-ignoring) instead of a mirror; a periodic full checksum sweep reconciles drift
key_decisions.rationale: The mirror already carries exact change sets — deriving reindex from commits is deterministic and free; the watcher mode keeps the OSS single-box story working without git plumbing; the sweep is the tripwire for missed events

key_decisions.decision: Index plane: IndexStore driver interface with SQLite as the stranger's default (sqlite-vec vectors + FTS5 BM25 + plain relational claim/link edge tables — one index.db file) and a postgres driver (pgvector + tsvector + identical edge DDL) for split deployments; Alex's instance selects postgres on managed-postgres; LanceDB and Kùzu are both dropped
key_decisions.rationale: One embedded file gives the 15-minute quickstart and avoids the RAGFlow heavyweight trap; Postgres solves ADR-0029's cross-host problem (G11 queries pve) for ALL THREE retrieval legs in one network service — the dossier flagged that Kùzu has the identical embedded-file cross-host defect ADR-0029 caught for LanceDB, and at personal scale (≤100k nodes) supports/refutes + one-hop expansion are trivial SQL (recursive CTEs), so a graph DB is a dependency without a payoff

key_decisions.decision: Hybrid retrieval: weighted Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) over BM25 + vector legs, then one-hop typed graph expansion of top-N seeds (edge-type weights: refutes surfaced explicitly for find_contradicting), optional CPU reranker (bge-reranker-v2-m3) as an off-by-default compose profile; every result carries a citation (note path + heading anchor + checksum-at-index-time)
key_decisions.rationale: Meets the SurfSense two-tier bar without mandating reranker compute; checksum-stamped citations make stale-answer detection mechanical; find_contradicting stays the unmatched differentiator as a pure graph query

key_decisions.decision: Events transport: Alex's instance runs the Curio webhook sink → kp-events receiver on CT 207 as primary, with the local file sink retained for replay/backfill; the OSS default is file-sink-only (local, private); events are NEVER committed to the canonical vault (.curio/events/ gitignored) and the interest model consumes them as derived signals; per-sink cursor files live in KP state dirs outside git
key_decisions.rationale: Behavioral reading data in a git repo is a privacy liability for the OSS story and unbounded append is bad git hygiene; events are signals (rebuildable posture: loss costs model fidelity, not knowledge) — canonical/derived rule applied honestly. The manifest, by contrast, IS committed because wipe-and-reinstall reconciliation depends on it

key_decisions.decision: Digest = vault note first, feed second: the discovery loop emits ONE digests/YYYY-MM-DD.md via proposals (durable, git-native, Obsidian-readable), and kp-sync renders digests/ into an Atom feed on G11 (kp-digest-feed/v1: stable tag:-URI GUIDs, full-content entries); notifier is a plugin (none/stdout/webhook/push-notifier) with push-notifier-LAN-ping as Alex's instance choice; Curio subscribes to the feed once its W1 per-feed private-network allowlist lands (filed in the schema review), closing digest→read/star/promote→events→interest-model inside the reader
key_decisions.rationale: ADR-0028 forbids cloud push and makes push-notifier pull-only, so the durable artifact must be the note; the feed makes the librarian's output a first-class reading-surface item at near-zero KP cost (R1 verdict: highest depth, lowest complexity); R4 read-later injection is deferred post-v1 (W5) since a digest item + one keystroke covers ~80%; R2 now.md stays KP-internal; R6 webhook-wakes-maintenance-loop and R7 OPML-to-vault cron both ship (zero Curio changes)

key_decisions.decision: Language: Python 3.12+ uv workspace replaces the scaffold's implied Node/npm-workspaces; pydantic models emit the published JSON Schema artifacts (mirroring Curio's schemars pattern); the librarian stays harness-level (markdown prompts/skills + scheduler + two adapters: Claude Code reference, OpenAI-compatible loop)
key_decisions.rationale: Every OCR engine RFC-0018 names is Python, FastMCP is the mature server SDK, all store clients are first-class Python, and the Curio seam is file contracts — no FFI ever; Node would force strangers to install two runtimes. Harness-level librarian means the OSS system requires no Claude subscription, mirroring the Obsidian recommended-not-required posture

key_decisions.decision: Three-repo split with fresh product re-scaffold: GitHub-canonical product repo (Apache-2.0 or MIT OR Apache-2.0, renamed before publication) pulled into git-forge via RFC-0016 Mode A; alex/knowledge-plane repurposed as the private instance repo; the canonical vault gets its own private repo and absorbs now.md; Docling (MIT) is the only default OCR engine, AGPL/GPL engines optional subprocess-isolated extras
key_decisions.rationale: The scaffold conflates product, instance, and knowledge (vault/, now.md, LAN URLs, TODO(alex) inside the would-be-public repo) and predates the ADR-0029 amendment in three files — one throwaway commit makes re-scaffold cheaper than surgery; AGPL is documented adoption friction (Khoj) and Curio's own licensing reasoning applies: open contracts, not copyleft, are the lock-in defense; PyMuPDF4LLM(AGPL)/Marker(GPL-class) in the default path would poison a permissive license

key_decisions.decision: RSS ownership: Curio is the primary RSS path (frontmatter-v1 promotion + events-v1 signals); kp-ingest ships a deliberately minimal feeds-fallback producer emitting kp-note/v1 notes for users without Curio; the KP never builds a second full reader pipeline
key_decisions.rationale: Two fetchers with divergent normalization is the failure the dossier names; but 'bring Curio or bring markdown' is too thin for OSS standalone adoption — a minimal fallback keeps the quickstart self-sufficient while the integration guide points at Curio (or any frontmatter-v1-conforming producer) for the real reading loop

key_decisions.decision: KP files Curio's schema review NOW (it is consumer #1 and the gate is mandatory): findings = (1) negation events missing (unstarred/unread/read_later_removed — winner's enum is monotone-only, breaking state reconstruction and interest-model decay), (2) no event_id ULID in the JSONL projection (needed for dedupe/exactly-once across file+webhook sinks; the monolith variant had it), (3) article payload lacks tags (topic signal at event time), (4) ts format + ordering + cursor opacity unspecified, (5) stable schema $id URLs + machine-fetchable registry location (W3), (6) W1 private-network per-feed allowlist, (7) managed-region marker reservation in frontmatter-v1.md docs (W2), (8) digest-feed convention documented as a supported pattern (W6); review lives as a document in the KP product repo docs/ plus a Curio-repo issue
key_decisions.rationale: Curio's plan says a v0 identity/semantics mistake becomes a breaking change with a real downstream the moment the KP wires in — the review is load-bearing and months of lead time exist now; filing it in both repos keeps it discoverable from either side

integration_fabric.contracts:
PUBLISHED BY THE KP (all semver'd JSON Schema artifacts generated from pydantic in CI with a drift gate; stable $id URLs under the product domain /schemas/<name>/v<major>/; additive-in-minors, breaking-in-majors; per-schema CHANGELOG; COMPAT.md matrix):
1. kp-note/v1 — the note frontmatter family. core profile (required: title, schema_version, checksum [bare hex sha256 of normalized body, normalization documented: LF, trimmed trailing whitespace], one identity field, saved or published); curio-compat profile = frontmatter-v1 verbatim (validation delegates to the pinned Curio schema); zotero profile (adds zotero_key required, citekey, doi, authors, origin: zotero); web/feed/manual profiles (kp_id required, origin enum, source URL); digest profile (digest date, thread refs, item list with note_id citations). Ownership: KP owns core+zotero+web+digest; Curio owns curio-compat by reference. status is NOT a field anywhere — lifecycle is index-side.
2. kp-digest-feed/v1 — Atom convention: one entry per digest, GUID = tag:<vault-id>,<date>:digest (stable forever), full content:encoded HTML rendered from the digest note, category per thread; documented so any agent can publish and any conforming reader (Curio post-W1) can subscribe.
3. managed-region/v1 — <!-- kp:begin owner=<component> section=<name> v=1 --> … <!-- kp:end -->: single-writer-per-region rule, nesting forbidden, gate-validated integrity; reserved with Curio for future in-note enrichment and highlights.
4. MCP tool contracts (stdio default; stateless Streamable HTTP per the 2026-07-28 RC for network deployment, bearer-token auth): kp-retrieval-mcp v1 (search_hybrid(query,k,filters{origin,tags,date}), find_contradicting(claim|note_id), unlinked_neighbors(note_id), get_note(note_id), list_recent(n)); kp-vault-mcp v1 (list_notes, read_note, search_text, propose_patch(branch,files) — the ONLY write verb, lands on proposals/*); kp-zotero-mcp v1 (search_items, get_item(key), get_fulltext(key), get_annotations(key), add_item(doi|url|bibtex — web-API write)).
5. kp-events/v1 — internal bus (JSONL + webhook): vault.updated{old,new,changed[]}, note.ingested{note_id,path,checksum}, index.updated{note_ids,model}, digest.published{date,path}, curio.event{passthrough} — the glue between mirror (G11) and indexer/librarian (pve), and the OSS automation surface.
6. proposals/v1 branch convention — proposals/<agent>/<date>-<slug>; agent credentials can push only proposals/* (remote branch protection); deterministic CI gate on the vault repo validates: per-profile frontmatter schema, checksum correctness for KP-produced notes, path allowlist (inbox/, literature/, notes/re/, digests/), zero diffs on manifest-listed (Curio-owned) paths, wikilink integrity, managed-region integrity; merge = human (or explicitly delegated deterministic auto-merge for digest notes only, off by default).
7. Zotero WebDAV attachment-cache contract — derived cache layout attachments/<itemKey>/<propHash>/… with extraction manifest; vendored .prop/.zip parser covered by contract tests acting as an upstream-format-drift tripwire (the format is officially unsupported).

CONSUMED BY THE KP (pinned in contracts/curio-pins.toml and friends):
1. Curio frontmatter-v1 + events-v1 — pinned $id+version+sha256; validated at ingest and in CI (cross-repo contract job pulls Curio's released artifacts and validates KP fixtures); additive minors tolerated (unknown fields preserved), major mismatch → quarantine dir + notifier alert, never partial ingest; .curio/manifest.json consumed read-only as the ownership oracle; events cursors persisted per sink (file sink: rotation-aware byte/ULID cursor; webhook: last-seen id) in derived state dirs.
2. Zotero Web API v3 — metadata deltas via ?since=<Last-Modified-Version>, If-Modified-Since-Version etiquette, backoff on 429; API key scoped read (write scope only if add_item enabled); key in secrets-manager/Keychain (instance) or env/_FILE (OSS).
3. Zotero WebDAV store — nginx dav_ext with full GET/PUT/DELETE/PROPFIND/MKCOL/OPTIONS (Zotero-tested combo), basic-auth htpasswd; KEY.prop/KEY.zip pair semantics honored (orphan .zip = corruption signal, surfaced not auto-fixed).
4. OpenAI-compatible LLM + embeddings endpoints (/v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings) — base URL + key + model as config; default localhost Ollama; the index records embedding model+dims+version and refuses queries on mismatch (embedding swap = deliberate migration with reindex).
5. Better BibTeX Extra-field citekey convention ('Citation Key: <key>') — read-only.
6. Git remote API (branch protection + webhook) — needs only: protected main, agent-scoped push to proposals/*, push webhook; git-forge/GitHub/Gitea all qualify (pluggable).
VERSIONING STORY: every contract carries schema_version in-band where it has instances (notes, events) and $id-versioned artifacts out-of-band; consumers pin exact versions with hashes; the schema-review gate with Curio runs before ANY consumer code ships; a field change anywhere in kp-note is a migration with a written migration note in the schema CHANGELOG — the scaffold schema's own rule, now enforced by the CI drift gate.
integration_fabric.alex_instance_vs_oss: The OSS system is everything in the product repo: packages, contracts, vault-template, reference compose (all-in-one default: single box, SQLite index, file-watcher reindex, Ollama-localhost embeddings, file-sink events, stdout/none notifier, stdio MCP, any markdown+YAML directory as vault, optional minimal feeds-fallback producer, Docling-only OCR). Alex's instance is exclusively configuration + placement in the private instance repo, zero forked code: (1) LLM/embeddings → llm-gateway CT 106 (192.0.2.5) with a librarian virtual key, observability → a new llm-observability 'librarian' project (CT 107) — OSS equivalent: any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, no observability requirement; (2) IndexStore driver → postgres against managed-postgres CT 111 (schema-per-app tenancy via managed-postgres-schema-add.sh; pgvector presence verified by one-liner before Phase 3) — OSS default: sqlite driver, or any Postgres 15+ with pgvector; (3) deployment → split profile across the fleet per ADR-0029: CT 206 zotero (WebDAV + kp-zotero-mcp), new CT 207 kp-access (mirror + vault/retrieval MCPs + digest feed + events receiver) behind the existing caddy ingress on G11; kp-worker VM on pve (OCR/embedding/indexer/librarian, claude-agent pattern, RFC-0013 NFS worktree) — OSS default: one compose stack; (4) git remote → private git-forge (vault repo + instance repo), product repo GitHub-canonical mirrored in via RFC-0016 Mode A — OSS: any git host with branch protection + webhooks, or watcher-mode with no remote at all; (5) MCP transport → Streamable HTTP behind caddy with bearer tokens (G11 network deployment) — OSS default stdio in .mcp.json; (6) notifier → push-notifier CT 203 LAN-only pull ping (ADR-0028-conformant) — OSS default none/stdout, webhook available; (7) events → Curio webhook sink to CT 207 primary + Mac file sink for replay — OSS default file sink only; (8) librarian harness → Claude Code on the pve agent-VM pattern with cron — OSS: either adapter, cron/systemd, budget/turn caps; (9) secrets → Keychain canonical + secrets-manager escrow + host-only token files — OSS: env vars + _FILE docker-secrets; (10) instance data → canonical vault repo (with now.md) and behavioral events NEVER enter the product repo; (11) IaC → instance repo pins the product repo at a release SHA and the management plane vendors guest roles per RFC-0014/ADR-0022 — OSS users get compose, not Ansible. Litmus test shipped as CI: `just demo` on a clean machine (seeded sample vault + sample feed + Ollama) must run ingest→index→search_hybrid end-to-end with zero references to any 192.0.2.x host, llm-gateway, llm-observability, managed-postgres, git-forge, push-notifier, or Obsidian.
risks: Zotero WebDAV .prop/.zip format is officially unsupported and undocumented — an upstream format change silently breaks the fulltext shim; mitigated by vendored-parser contract tests as a tripwire and quarantine-not-crash behavior, but residual risk is real and the extraction cache must surface (never auto-delete) corruption signals like orphan .zip files

Curio contract timing: frontmatter-v1/events-v1 ship at Curio Phase 3 (~11-14.5 weeks out) and the name/trademark clearance (Zengobi collision) blocks public integration docs — the KP must sequence Zotero + web-clip + fallback-feeds value first and keep the curio adapter behind a feature flag until the schema review completes; if the review findings (negation events, event_id, tags-in-payload) are rejected, the interest model degrades to monotone signals and cross-sink dedupe needs heuristic (ts+curio_id) keys

The bidirectional loop hard-depends on Curio W1 (per-feed private-network allowlist in PolicedClient): if it doesn't land, Curio cannot subscribe to the LAN digest feed and the loop falls back to digest-note-in-Obsidian + push-notifier ping — file W1 early and design the digest note to be fully self-sufficient

pve real utilization is still unmeasured (ADR-0029 precondition) and the index-plane design adds Postgres index load to managed-postgres plus OCR/embedding/librarian on the same host — measure before Phase 3, with the sanctioned fallback being reduced librarian cadence, not new hardware; pgvector presence in the pinned managed-postgres PG15 image is still an unverified one-liner

G11 headroom numbers predate CT 206, the proposed CT 207, and the router-fw cutover (VM 200 claims 2c/4GB when routing) — re-derive free cores/RAM before creating CT 207, and note the cutover window itself (scheduled ~2026-07-01, unconfirmed in logs) interacts with CT 206's staged gw/DNS params

Ownership-boundary regressions are the highest-probability integration bug class: any KP component writing a manifest-listed path, or a vault git hook/normalizer touching Curio-owned notes, corrupts the reconcile story — the vault-repo CI gate and the mirror's read-only credential are the machinery, but local-Mac tooling (log-normalize-style hooks) must also exempt manifest-listed paths

Checksum semantics depend on Curio's body-normalization algorithm which the KP does not reimplement — treated as an opaque change token, which means the KP cannot VERIFY Curio note integrity, only detect change; acceptable, but must be documented so nobody 'fixes' it into a brittle re-computation

Licensing landmines: PyMuPDF4LLM (AGPL) and Marker (GPL-class) must never enter the default dependency tree of the permissive product repo — subprocess isolation + extras-only installation is the design, and CI needs a license-audit gate to keep it true; 'knowledge-plane' must be renamed with trademark/domain clearance before anything goes public

Embedding-model lock-in: the index is versioned against one model (self-describing metadata, refuse-on-mismatch) — a model swap is a full deliberate reindex, and CPU-only throughput on the M5 Ultra for the initial corpus embed is unbenchmarked; batch windows and llm-gateway routing to a remote model are the instance-side escape valves

Split-brain between file-watcher mode and git-mirror mode: all-in-one users index the working tree (uncommitted changes visible) while split deployments index HEAD of the mirror (committed only) — document the semantic difference explicitly; the periodic checksum sweep bounds drift in both modes

The webhook events receiver on CT 207 is a new always-on ingest surface on the LAN — bearer-token auth and body-schema validation required even inside the LAN (deny-by-default posture matching the house's push-notifier/Caddy discipline)

Instance/product drift: Alex's split-profile deployment exercises code paths (postgres driver, HTTP MCP, webhook events) that the OSS default (sqlite, stdio, file sink) doesn't, and vice versa — CI must run the matrix both ways or one leg rots
rfc0018_amendments: §Index 'full-text/BM25 (free via the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch)' — AMEND: FTS is system-owned (SQLite FTS5 default / Postgres tsvector in the postgres driver); the Obsidian-plugin path died with ADR-0029's live-desktop-app removal and violates the Obsidian-never-a-dependency distributability constraint

§Access 'a Zotero server (reads zotero.sqlite directly, no live app needed)' and the placement table's 'Zotero MCP reads a WebDAV-synced local copy of the library' — AMEND: zotero.sqlite never reaches G11 and WebDAV carries only attachments as .prop/.zip pairs; the data path is two-channel (zotero.org Web API metadata deltas + WebDAV extraction shim for fulltext), colocated on CT 206 — placement unchanged, mechanism respecified

§Access 'an Obsidian server (needs the live desktop app)' body text — AMEND to match ADR-0029's already-accepted resolution: kp-vault-mcp reads the git mirror off disk; the RFC body was never re-edited after the amendment

§Alternatives 'mature MCP options exist... only the retrieval-fusion server is genuinely custom' — AMEND: under the headless design, the Zotero MCP (web-API + WebDAV-shim) and the vault MCP are both built in-repo; the named candidates (sqlite-reading Zotero servers, coddingtonbear/cyanheads live-app servers) don't fit the accepted topology; only Mac-side desktop use of third-party servers remains recommended

§Ingestion RSS fetch — AMEND: Curio (frontmatter-v1 promotion + events-v1 signals) is the primary RSS/reading path; the KP's built-in fetcher is demoted to a minimal fallback producer for non-Curio users; Phase 2 rescoped to Zotero + web-clip + fallback-feeds

§Ingestion frontmatter list (title, source, zotero_key, doi, tags, status, checksum) and the scaffold's 'locked' packages/shared/frontmatter-schema.md — AMEND/SUPERSEDE: the kp-note/v1 family (Curio-anchored: source=URL, schema_version, saved/published, bare-hex checksum, origin enum as a KP extension) replaces it, and `status` leaves frontmatter entirely — lifecycle state is index-side keyed on producer-namespaced note_id (this is the migration the schema doc's own rule requires)

Placement table 'push-notifier digest delivery' + §Librarian 'emits one digest' — AMEND per ADR-0028: the digest's primary artifact is the digest note itself (digests/YYYY-MM-DD.md via proposals), rendered as a stable-GUID Atom feed on G11 that the reading surface subscribes to; push-notifier is an optional LAN-only pull ping in Alex's instance; the notifier is a pluggable abstraction (none/stdout/webhook/push-notifier) in the OSS system — no cloud push ever

§Index graph store (brief's Kùzu inheritance) — AMEND: the claim/link graph lives as relational edge tables inside the same network-reachable index store as vectors and FTS (SQLite tables in the default driver, Postgres tables in Alex's), extending ADR-0029's own cross-host reasoning from the vector store to the graph leg; no embedded graph-DB file

NEW SCOPE section (or companion RFC): distributability — the product/instance/vault three-repo split, GitHub-canonical product repo under a new name with Apache-2.0 (or MIT OR Apache-2.0) licensing, RFC-0016 Mode A mirror into the estate, pluggable LLM gateway/embedder/index driver/git remote/notifier, Python uv workspace replacing the '@plane/*' Node naming, vault/ and now.md evicted from the code monorepo into the canonical vault, Docling-only default OCR with copyleft engines as isolated extras; ADR-0029 itself requires NO amendment — every placement it decides becomes instance-side configuration under the split, which validates the decision

mcp_agent.summary: MCP + agent architecture for the Knowledge Plane. THE MCP FLEET is three in-repo servers, all FastMCP (Python SDK), dual-transport (stdio default for OSS/localhost; stateless Streamable HTTP for network deployment per the MCP 2026-07-28 RC), all published to the MCP Registry: (1) kp-zotero — two-channel Zotero access: metadata via zotero.org Web API (pyzotero, Last-Modified-Version polling + local cache), fulltext/PDF bytes via a shim that unzips the co-located WebDAV .prop/.zip store into a derived attachment cache (this corrects RFC-0018's nonexistent 'reads zotero.sqlite'/'WebDAV-synced library copy' data path — WebDAV carries attachments only, and zotero.org cannot serve WebDAV-stored files, so only the WebDAV host can serve them: ADR-0029's CT 206 co-location is strengthened, not amended). Tool surface: zotero_search_items, zotero_get_item, zotero_recent(since_version), zotero_get_fulltext, zotero_get_pdf, zotero_get_annotations, zotero_get_collections, zotero_items_in_collection, zotero_get_by_doi, zotero_search_by_citekey (optional/BBT), plus flag-gated writes (zotero_add_by_identifier into a 'KP Proposed' staging collection only). (2) kp-vault — filesystem-direct over the git-mirrored vault (Obsidian never required): list_notes, get_note, get_frontmatter, get_backlinks, get_tags, list_recent, list_inbox(status), grep_notes, get_manifest_ownership, and the ONLY write path any agent has: propose_note/propose_patch/open_proposals — commits on proposals/* branches pushed to the remote, with server-side refusal to touch Curio-manifest-owned paths and no direct-to-main tool existing at all. (3) kp-retrieval — the custom differentiator over the derived indices: search_hybrid (BM25+vector+one-hop-graph fusion with per-leg score breakdown + note/heading citations), search_fulltext, find_contradicting, find_supporting, unlinked_neighbors, related_by_citation, get_claim_graph, similar_to_text (dedupe primitive), index_status (model/version/staleness — the index is self-describing). Auth for HTTP: static bearer tokens, two scopes (read / propose), minted per consumer, TLS at the ingress; OSS default binds 127.0.0.1. Alex's instance: kp-zotero on CT 206, kp-vault+kp-retrieval+vault-mirror sidecar+digest.xml emitter on a new G11 CT (207), path-routed behind Caddy at https://kp.example.internal/mcp/{zotero,vault,retrieval}, reachable from the Mac and agent VMs; tokens in Keychain/secrets-manager. THE LIBRARIAN: personal instance = headless Claude Code on cron in a new VM on pve (claude-agent/pi-agent clone), ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointed at llm-gateway CT 106 (virtual key 'kp-librarian' with budget) so llm-observability tracing/cost lands in a 'librarian' project for free; OSS = the same prompts (plain markdown) + .claude/skills + a thin provider-neutral tool-loop runner ('kp agent run maintenance|discovery') speaking any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama default) with the three MCP servers bridged — Claude Code is the recommended harness, never required (mirrors the Obsidian posture). Maintenance loop (cron + Curio events-v1 webhook trigger on G11): drain inbox → OCR-route (deterministic kp CLI jobs the agent supervises) → incremental embed keyed on checksum → dedupe via similar_to_text → propose literature-note stubs and companion notes (never in-place edits to Curio-owned files) on one dated proposals/maintenance-* branch → 1-line summary. Discovery loop (digest cadence): read in-vault now.md → deterministic interest-model rebuild (thread vectors = embedding of thread prose + evidence-note centroid; per-claim vectors; evidence weights = source-type priors × exponential recency decay, half-life 30d default; inspectable JSON artifact + a rendered interest-model report) → rank new-since-last-digest candidates; Curio events (promoted>starred>read_later>read) apply ONLY as multiplicative re-rank boosts on candidates already inside a now.md thread's scope — events never create threads, never expand scope, never write canonically → emit ONE digest or nothing. Digest delivery reconciles the stale push-notifier assumption against ADR-0028: primary artifact is the digest note (digests/YYYY-MM-DD.md via an auto-mergeable proposals/digest-* branch), second surface is a locally-served Atom feed (stable GUIDs, static file on G11 Caddy) that Curio subscribes to once Curio W1 (PolicedClient LAN allowlist) lands — closing the read/star/promote→events→interest-model circle inside the reading surface — plus an optional LAN-only push-notifier 1-liner; OSS notifier is pluggable (none|file|webhook|push-notifier|command) and the Atom emitter is built-in. Safety = GitHub-Agentic-Workflows 'safe outputs' separation enforced at three layers: prompt (CLAUDE.md standing orders), tool (kp-vault exposes proposal writes only + manifest-ownership refusal), credential (librarian's git token can push only proposals/* refs, cannot merge/force-push/delete; a separately-credentialed deterministic CI validator on the vault repo gates schema/links/path-allowlist and alone may auto-merge digest/stub path classes). AGENT-FACING DX (the product headline 'your agent can use your library'): kp init scaffolds config + a vault-side .claude/ starter (CLAUDE.md + /research,/library,/contradictions skills); kp mcp install writes stdio .mcp.json entries; examples/ ships localhost and network .mcp.json variants; any MCP client works — 15 minutes from clone to an agent answering from your own notes with citations.
mcp_agent.layout:
THREE-REPO SPLIT (product / instance / knowledge), per the distribution baseline:

kp/  — PUBLIC PRODUCT REPO (GitHub-canonical, name TBD pre-publication; RFC-0016 Mode A reference mirror back into git-forge mirrors/)
├── pyproject.toml            # Python 3.12+ uv workspace (reverses the scaffold's empty npm-workspaces implication)
├── justfile                  # setup/up/down/demo/ingest/reindex/mcp/librarian-once/digest-once/test/lint/schema-check
├── .claude/                  # shipped product surface: CLAUDE.md (canonical/derived rule, proposal-only doctrine) + skills (/ingest-run /reindex /propose /digest /schema-bump)
├── .github/workflows/        # lint/type/test + cross-repo contract job validating fixtures against Curio's published frontmatter-v1/events-v1 schema artifacts
├── packages/
│   ├── shared/               # pydantic models → emitted JSON Schemas: kp-frontmatter-v2, config schema, pinned Curio schemas, tool-surface types
│   ├── ingest/               # Zotero poller (since_version), web-clip, curio adapter (frontmatter-v1 → index records keyed on curio_id), OCR router (Docling MIT default; PyMuPDF4LLM/Marker as subprocess-isolated copyleft extras), fallback RSS fetcher (demoted)
│   ├── index/                # chunker, embedding client (OpenAI-compatible HTTP, Ollama default), FTS driver (SQLite FTS5 default | pg tsvector), vector driver (sqlite-vec default | pgvector), graph driver (Kuzu embedded default | pg edges table), checksum-keyed incremental watcher, index metadata (self-describing model/version)
│   ├── mcp-zotero/           # FastMCP server: web-API metadata channel + WebDAV .prop/.zip fulltext shim + derived attachment cache; attachment backend pluggable: webdav-local | zotero-cloud | local-dir
│   ├── mcp-vault/            # FastMCP server: git-mirror reads, grep, inbox listing, manifest-ownership check, propose_* writes (the only agent write path); mirror-sync sidecar (interval pull + post-receive webhook)
│   ├── mcp-retrieval/        # FastMCP server: search_hybrid/find_contradicting/find_supporting/unlinked_neighbors/related_by_citation/get_claim_graph/similar_to_text/search_fulltext/index_status over the index drivers
│   └── librarian/            # prompts/*.md (harness-agnostic), interest model (build + inspect), digest composer + Atom emitter, notifier plugins (none|file|webhook|push-notifier|command), 'kp agent' neutral OpenAI-compatible tool-loop, Curio events ingester (file-sink cursor + webhook receiver), run ledger
├── fleet/compose.yml         # reference deployment; COMPOSE_PROFILES: core (all-in-one embedded) | mcp | index | postgres | ollama; CPU-only images
├── examples/mcp/             # localhost.mcp.json (stdio/uvx), network.mcp.json (HTTP + ${KP_MCP_TOKEN}), vault-starter .claude/
├── docs/                     # quickstart (just demo), contracts/, curio-integration.md (digest-feed convention, manifest ownership), agents.md ('point your agent at your library'), licensing (OCR extras)
└── config.example.toml + .env.example   # complete stranger config surface; secrets via env/_FILE refs only

alex/knowledge-plane  — PRIVATE INSTANCE REPO (existing git-forge repo repurposed; never mirrored out)
├── config/kp.toml            # real endpoints: llm-gateway 192.0.2.5, managed-postgres pgvector DSN, WebDAV CT 206, kp.example.internal, push-notifier
├── compose.g11-mcp.yml / compose.pve-index.yml / compose.librarian.yml   # ADR-0029 placement as pure config overlay
├── .mcp.json                 # network variant: https://kp.example.internal/mcp/{zotero,vault,retrieval}, bearer from Keychain helper
├── product pin               # kp product repo at a release SHA (submodule or locked pip index), RFC-0014/ADR-0022 style
└── secrets refs only         # Keychain canonical, secrets-manager escrow; tokens: mcp-read, mcp-propose, librarian git push-token

vault (NEW private repo — the canonical knowledge store; Obsidian is the recommended viewer)
├── inbox/ notes/ digests/ raw/          # digests/ is the agent-owned auto-mergeable namespace
├── now.md                               # moves here from the code repo — hand-authored steering state is canonical
├── .curio/ (manifest.json, events/)     # Curio-owned; committed (manifest required for reconcile; events JSONL in-vault for Alex — OSS default keeps events OUT of git in a data dir)
├── .claude/                             # vault-starter: CLAUDE.md + /research /library /contradictions skills (scaffolded by kp init --claude)
└── .git-forge|.github workflows/          # the DISPOSAL SIDE: deterministic proposal-branch validator (frontmatter schema, link integrity, path allowlist, Curio-owned-file refusal); the only identity with merge rights over proposals/*

GUEST PLACEMENT (Alex instance, ADR-0029-conformant): CT 206 zotero (WebDAV nginx + kp-mcp-zotero); NEW CT 207 kp-mcp on G11 (kp-mcp-vault + kp-mcp-retrieval + vault mirror + digest.xml → Caddy CT 205 ingress + the Curio-webhook trigger shim); librarian VM on pve (claude-agent pattern, RFC-0013 workspace for the proposals worktree); index jobs + Ollama embeddings on pve; vector/FTS in managed-postgres pgvector (pending the one-line extension check).

key_decisions.decision: Build all three MCP servers in-repo (kp-zotero, kp-vault, kp-retrieval) rather than adopting 54yyyu/zotero-mcp or any Obsidian-plugin server
key_decisions.rationale: The headless two-channel Zotero path (web-API metadata + WebDAV zip fulltext) exists nowhere off the shelf; 54yyyu's built-in ChromaDB semantic search would create a second derived embedding store violating single-index discipline; every mature Obsidian MCP either needs the live desktop app (forbidden by ADR-0029 and the OSS no-Obsidian-dependency rule) or lacks the proposal-write contract. A distributable product needs pinned, license-compatible, contract-controlled servers; pyzotero and FastMCP keep each thin. This amends RFC-0018's 'never build from scratch' alternative.

key_decisions.decision: kp-zotero is two-channel: zotero.org Web API for metadata (Last-Modified-Version polling, local cache), a WebDAV .prop/.zip unzip shim with a derived attachment cache for fulltext/PDF bytes; attachment backend pluggable (webdav-local | zotero-cloud | local-dir)
key_decisions.rationale: WebDAV sync carries ONLY attachments in an undocumented zip format and zotero.org's API cannot serve WebDAV-stored files — RFC-0018's 'reads zotero.sqlite' and 'WebDAV-synced library copy' data paths do not exist headless. Only the WebDAV host can serve those bytes, which strengthens CT 206 co-location. The pluggable backend lets OSS users on Zotero File Storage or a same-machine data dir skip WebDAV entirely — and this niche (headless WebDAV fulltext) is a differentiator nobody serves.

key_decisions.decision: kp-vault reads a git-mirrored vault off disk and exposes proposal-branch writes as the ONLY write primitive (propose_note/propose_patch); no direct-write tool exists; server refuses paths listed in .curio/manifest.json
key_decisions.rationale: Implements ADR-0029's git-mirror decision and RFC-0018's proposals-only rule at the tool layer, not just in prompts; Curio re-promotion re-renders whole files from template, so any KP write to a Curio-owned note would be silently clobbered — librarian enrichment lands in companion notes that wikilink the Curio note (R3a). Obsidian stays a viewer, never a dependency.

key_decisions.decision: All servers: FastMCP dual-transport — stdio default (OSS localhost, .mcp.json single-user), stateless Streamable HTTP for network deployment, built against the MCP 2026-07-28 RC (no protocol sessions) while supporting 2025-06-18 stable
key_decisions.rationale: OSS users get zero-config stdio; Alex needs cross-host reach (Mac + agent VMs → G11 per ADR-0029). Statelessness lets servers sit behind ordinary Caddy ingress and survive the spec's July 28 finalization without rework.

key_decisions.decision: HTTP auth: static bearer tokens with two scopes (read / propose), one token per consumer, TLS at the Caddy ingress, 127.0.0.1 bind as OSS default; no OAuth in v1
key_decisions.rationale: Single-user system on a LAN — per-consumer tokens give revocation and audit without an identity provider; the propose scope isolates the one dangerous capability. MCP's OAuth story is noted as the multi-user future, not built now. Tokens live in Keychain/secrets-manager per house secrets discipline.

key_decisions.decision: Personal-instance librarian = headless Claude Code on cron in a new pve VM (claude-agent/pi-agent clone), ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL → llm-gateway with a budgeted 'kp-librarian' virtual key; OSS librarian = the identical plain-markdown prompts + skills driven by 'kp agent run', a thin provider-neutral OpenAI-compatible tool-loop (Ollama default) with the MCP servers bridged
key_decisions.rationale: Claude Code is the proven house harness (--allowedTools/--max-turns/budget triad, CLAUDE.md standing orders) and llm-gateway routing buys llm-observability tracing + cost accounting for free. The OSS system must not require an Anthropic subscription — prompts/skills/scheduler are the product, the harness is pluggable; the neutral runner ships v1-complete for the maintenance loop, experimental for discovery (higher judgment demands).

key_decisions.decision: Discovery-loop interest model: now.md (in-vault, hand-authored, canonical) defines threads; a deterministic job derives per-thread topic vectors (thread prose embedding + evidence-note centroid), per-claim vectors, and evidence weights (source-type priors × exponential recency decay, 30d half-life), persisted as an inspectable JSON artifact plus a rendered report; Curio events-v1 (promoted>starred>read_later>read) apply only as multiplicative re-rank boosts on candidates already matching a now.md thread — never creating threads, never expanding scope, never writing canonically
key_decisions.rationale: Keeps the model derived-but-inspectable (rebuildable, git-diffable report, Alex-editable steering) and encodes the directive that behavioral signal steers within explicit human intent rather than autonomously drifting. Monotone events stream (no negation events until Curio W4) means decay must be time-based. Cold start = zotero-arxiv-daily-style library-centroid similarity.

key_decisions.decision: Digest delivery: primary artifact is digests/YYYY-MM-DD.md landed via an auto-mergeable proposals/digest-* branch; second surface is a locally-served Atom feed with stable GUIDs (static file, G11 Caddy) that Curio subscribes to; optional LAN-only push-notifier 1-liner; OSS notifier pluggable (none|file|webhook|push-notifier|command)
key_decisions.rationale: Reconciles RFC-0018's stale 'push-notifier digest delivery' with ADR-0028 (push-notifier is LAN-only pull — a ping, not a delivery mechanism): the durable, git-native note is the digest; the Atom feed closes the full loop inside Curio (digest → read/star/promote → events → interest model) at near-zero KP cost, pending Curio W1 (PolicedClient LAN allowlist). Anything cloud-push (Telegram/Pushover) is forbidden by ADR-0028.

key_decisions.decision: Safety rails enforced at three independent layers — prompt (CLAUDE.md standing orders: proposals-only, one-digest-or-silence, contradiction-first, never touch Curio-owned files), tool (kp-vault has no direct-write or destructive git capability at all), credential (librarian git token pushes only proposals/* refs, cannot merge/force-push/delete) — with a separately-credentialed deterministic CI validator on the vault repo as the disposal side (schema, link integrity, path allowlist), the only identity permitted to auto-merge digest/stub path classes
key_decisions.rationale: GitHub Agentic Workflows' proven doctrine: 'the agent proposes, a different process disposes — that separation is the whole security model.' Prompt-only guardrails are not rails; the KP already owns the CI-gate idiom in the management plane. If git-forge's branch protection can't express push-only-to-proposals/*, tool + CI layers still hold the invariant.

key_decisions.decision: Maintenance loop is agent-supervised deterministic machinery, not agent-improvised: OCR routing, incremental embedding, and index updates are kp CLI jobs keyed on frontmatter checksum; the agent drains the inbox, triages OCR failures to a review queue, runs dedupe via similar_to_text, and writes stub/companion proposals; loop triggers on cron plus Curio's events-v1 webhook (promoted events wake the drain)
key_decisions.rationale: House doctrine — deterministic tooling over agentic 'remember to run X'; LLM judgment is spent only where it pays (dedupe calls, stub prose, failure triage). Webhook triggering (R6, zero Curio changes) makes promoted articles appear indexed within minutes without polling waste. Low-confidence OCR never silently enters the claim graph (brief risk #4).

key_decisions.decision: Lifecycle state for Curio-owned notes lives index-side keyed on curio_id, never as a status field written into the note; KP-owned notes keep frontmatter status
key_decisions.rationale: Re-promotion clobbers any injected frontmatter; curio_id is the manifest-tracked rename-stable identity. This resolves the schema collision operationally: KP accepts vanilla frontmatter-v1 via an ingest adapter (never a custom Curio template on Alex's instance — his install must stay representative of the OSS story).

key_decisions.decision: Retrieval owns its own FTS leg: SQLite FTS5 embedded default, Postgres tsvector co-located with pgvector on Alex's instance; graph gets the same driver treatment (Kuzu embedded default, Postgres edges table for cross-host)
key_decisions.rationale: RFC-0018's 'BM25 free via Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch' died with the live-app dependency and violates the no-Obsidian-dependency rule. The driver-interface pattern (embedded default, network option) resolves LanceDB-vs-pgvector AND the never-asked Kùzu cross-host question with one seam: Alex selects network drivers because ADR-0029 splits MCP (G11) from indices (pve); strangers get a zero-infra single-container core — the anti-RAGFlow posture.

key_decisions.decision: .mcp.json conventions: stable server names kp-zotero/kp-vault/kp-retrieval; product ships examples/mcp/localhost.mcp.json (stdio, uvx) and network.mcp.json (HTTP + ${KP_MCP_TOKEN}); 'kp mcp install' writes entries; 'kp init --claude' scaffolds a vault-side .claude/ starter (CLAUDE.md + /research /library /contradictions skills) so any Claude Code session opened in the vault has the full surface
key_decisions.rationale: The product headline — 'your agent can use your library' — must be a 15-minute experience: clone → uv sync → kp init → kp mcp install → agent answers from your notes with citations. Stable names let shipped skills reference tools portably; MCP Registry publication of all three servers is the discovery channel.

key_decisions.decision: Observability: Alex = llm-gateway virtual key + llm-observability 'librarian' project (per-agent-project house pattern) with zero librarian-side code; OSS = OTel GenAI spans from the neutral runner when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set, plus an always-on local JSONL run ledger ('kp librarian runs')
key_decisions.rationale: The gateway-callback pattern means observability is deployment config, not product code — exactly the pluggability the directive demands; the run ledger gives every user (and the digest's own footer) run/cost/proposal accounting without any external service.

key_decisions.decision: Curio schema review (KP files it, now): request negation events (or documented monotone semantics), tags in the event article payload, ts/ordering/cursor-opacity spec (W4); the LAN allowlist (W1) and managed-region reservation (W2) as digest-feed and future in-note-enrichment enablers
key_decisions.rationale: The KP is Curio's designated consumer #1 and the review gate is load-bearing on both sides — a wrong identity key or monotone-only stream is a breaking change once frozen; the interest model's decay signals and the digest-in-Curio loop both hang on these asks.

mcp_agent.contracts:
PUBLISHED BY THE KP (all as versioned JSON Schema artifacts emitted from pydantic in packages/shared, semver'd, additive-in-minors/breaking-in-majors, per-schema CHANGELOG, stable $id URLs — mirroring Curio's schemars pattern):
1. kp-tool-surface-v1 — the MCP tool contracts for kp-zotero/kp-vault/kp-retrieval (tool names, input/output schemas, error shapes, score-breakdown structure for search_hybrid, citation format: vault-relative path + heading anchor + zotero_key/doi where applicable). Tool names are API: renaming a tool is a major bump. Servers advertise contract version via an `about` resource.
2. kp-frontmatter-v2 — the reconciled note schema: adopts schema_version; accepts vanilla Curio frontmatter-v1 for Curio-owned notes (adapter maps source-URL/author/saved/curio_id → index records); KP-native notes carry source-kind (zotero|web|manual — rss retired to the Curio path), zotero_key, doi, status, prefixed checksum; identity keys formally declared: curio_id for Curio-sourced, zotero_key for Zotero-sourced, path+checksum otherwise. Frozen only after the Curio schema-review gate (KP's own rule: a field change is a migration).
3. proposals/* branch convention — branch naming (proposals/maintenance-YYYY-MM-DD, proposals/digest-YYYY-MM-DD), one-branch-per-run, commit-message format, the path-allowlist classes (digests/ auto-mergeable, inbox/notes stubs review-required), and the validator contract the vault-side CI implements (schema check, link integrity, allowlist, Curio-owned-file refusal). This is the KP's 'safe outputs' spec — any harness producing conforming branches is a valid librarian.
4. kp-digest-feed-v1 — Atom feed convention: stable per-digest GUIDs, one entry per digest, entry body = digest note HTML, served as a static file; documented so ANY agent digest (not just KP's) can feed ANY reader (pairs with Curio wish-list W6).
5. Interest-model artifact schema — the inspectable per-thread JSON (thread vector ref, claim vectors, evidence weights, decay params, last-build) so users can audit/diff steering state.
6. Config schema — config.toml (KP_* env overrides): vault path + optional git remote, Zotero block (API key/user-id, attachment backend), Curio seam (export dir, events sink path or webhook bind, cursor path), LLM + embedding endpoints (OpenAI-compatible; embedding model recorded in index metadata so the index is self-describing), FTS/vector/graph drivers, MCP transport/bind/tokens, librarian cadences, notifier.

CONSUMED BY THE KP (pinned, CI-enforced):
1. Curio frontmatter-v1 + events-v1 — vendored pinned JSON Schemas; every Curio-sourced note and JSONL line validated at ingest and in CI; a cross-repo contract job pulls Curio's released schema artifacts and validates KP parser fixtures (the enforcement mechanism for 'native, not bolt-on'); tolerate additive minors, hard-fail majors. Events cursor semantics honored per sink (file-sink rotation-aware; webhook receiver replay-idempotent). .curio/manifest.json read as the write-ownership oracle.
2. Zotero Web API v3 (rate-limit/backoff + Last-Modified-Version etiquette; API key scoped read-only by default) and the WebDAV .prop/.zip layout — officially unsupported upstream, so pinned by a format-probe integration test that fails loudly on drift, with zotero.org-fulltext fallback where the account allows.
3. MCP protocol 2025-06-18 stable + 2026-07-28 RC (stateless Streamable HTTP, Mcp-Method routing headers) — servers built stateless so the RC finalization is a version bump, not a rework.
4. OpenAI-compatible chat/embeddings API (the LLM/embedder seam — llm-gateway, Ollama, vLLM, llama.cpp all conform).
VERSIONING STORY: every published schema ships as a release artifact + docs page; the instance repo pins the product at release SHAs (RFC-0014/ADR-0022 style); index metadata records embedding model+schema versions so a mismatch forces a deliberate migration, never silent drift.
mcp_agent.alex_instance_vs_oss: The generic system is everything in the product repo: the five packages + three MCP servers, embedded-default drivers (SQLite FTS5, sqlite-vec, Kuzu), stdio-first transports bound to 127.0.0.1, any OpenAI-compatible LLM/embedding endpoint (Ollama localhost default), any markdown+git directory as the vault (Obsidian recommended viewer only), any git remote for proposals, pluggable notifier (none|file|webhook|push-notifier|command), harness-agnostic librarian (plain-markdown prompts + kp agent neutral runner; Claude Code recommended), OTel-or-JSONL observability, Docling-only OCR default (copyleft engines as user-installed subprocess extras), and the reference compose with profiles. Alex's instance differs ONLY by configuration and placement, held in the private instance repo: (1) transports flip to Streamable HTTP behind Caddy at kp.example.internal with per-consumer bearer tokens in Keychain/secrets-manager; (2) placement per ADR-0029 — kp-zotero co-located with WebDAV on CT 206, kp-vault/kp-retrieval + vault mirror + digest feed on new G11 CT 207, librarian VM + index/OCR/Ollama jobs on pve; (3) drivers flip to network: managed-postgres Postgres (pgvector + tsvector + graph edges) because MCP (G11) and index-builder (pve) are split cross-host; (4) LLM path is llm-gateway CT 106 with a budgeted kp-librarian virtual key, observability is llm-observability CT 107 'librarian' project via the gateway callback — zero librarian-side code difference; (5) harness is headless Claude Code on cron (claude-agent/pi-agent VM pattern, RFC-0013 workspace for the proposals worktree); (6) notifier enables the LAN-only push-notifier ping (ADR-0028-compliant) and the digest Atom feed on Caddy for Curio; (7) Zotero attachment backend = webdav-local against CT 206's store; (8) git remotes are git-forge (vault repo private; product repo is GitHub-canonical with an RFC-0016 Mode A reference mirror into git-forge mirrors/); (9) canonical data (vault, now.md, .curio/) lives in Alex's private vault repo — the product repo contains no instance data, no LAN IPs, no TODO(alex) narration (the current scaffold conflates all three and gets re-scaffolded fresh, which is cheaper than surgery on one throwaway commit). llm-gateway, llm-observability, managed-postgres, git-forge, push-notifier, G11/pve topology are HIS choices behind the seams — none is referenced anywhere in product code, and the acceptance test for that claim is the OSS quickstart: clone → uv sync → kp init → just demo on one laptop with none of his stack present.
risks: G11 headroom is stale: ADR-0029's '~2 cores/4-5 GB free' predates CT 206, the proposed CT 207 (vault+retrieval MCP, mirror, feed, webhook shim), and the router-fw cutover (VM 200 takes 2c/4GB when routing live) — re-derive before creating CT 207, and note the router cutover scheduled ~2026-07-01 has no confirming log entry, so CT 206/207 network params (gw 192.0.2.1) may be mid-transition.

The WebDAV .prop/.zip format is officially unsupported by Zotero upstream — a Zotero release could change it silently; mitigated by the format-probe test and zotero.org-fulltext fallback, but the fulltext shim is the system's most fragile dependency and a headline OSS feature.

The digest-in-Curio loop (highest-value integration) is blocked on Curio W1 (PolicedClient LAN allowlist) landing in Curio Phase 2/3 — months out and owned by a different plan; until then digest delivery is vault-note + optional push-notifier only.

Curio events-v1 is monotone (no unread/unstar/read-later-removal events) — interest-model decay is time-only until the W4 schema review lands negation events; if the review is skipped before schemas freeze, the gap becomes permanent (breaking change with a real downstream).

The provider-neutral librarian on small local models may underperform badly on the discovery loop (ranking judgment, digest composition) — risk of OSS users experiencing a worse product than the Claude Code reference; mitigated by keeping the deterministic share high (interest model, ranking, thresholds are code, not LLM) and labeling the neutral discovery adapter experimental.

git-forge/Git-host branch-protection granularity may not express 'push only proposals/* refs' cleanly — if not, the credential layer weakens to repo-wide push and the invariant rests on the tool + CI layers; verify per-host and document the degraded mode honestly in the OSS security docs.

pve real utilization is still unmeasured (ADR-0029 precondition) — OCR + Ollama embedding + librarian VM + existing agent guests may not fit; fallback is cadence/scope reduction, which degrades digest freshness, not correctness.

Static bearer tokens with no rotation machinery are the v1 auth story — acceptable single-user/LAN, but an OSS user exposing HTTP transports beyond localhost without the reverse-proxy+TLS guidance is one misconfiguration from an open library; ship secure-by-default binds and a loud docs warning.
MCP spec RC finalizes 2026-07-28 — building against the RC risks late-RC changes; dual-supporting 2025-06-18 stable adds test-matrix burden to a v1.

managed-postgres pgvector presence is still formally unverified (one-line check) and the PG15→17 migration is one-way and pending — index-driver work on Alex's instance should confirm both before Phase 3 commits.

Scope creep: three in-repo MCP servers + a neutral agent runner + an Atom emitter is materially more custom code than RFC-0018 budgeted ('only retrieval-fusion is custom') — each server must stay thin (pyzotero/FastMCP/gitpython do the lifting) or the personal-instance timeline slips.

Rename/licensing landmines before publication: 'knowledge-plane' collides with an established networking term; OCR extras (PyMuPDF4LLM AGPL, Marker GPL-class) must stay subprocess-isolated optional extras under an Apache-2.0/MIT product license, and the public repo needs the gitleaks gate the private plane never required.
rfc0018_amendments: §Design/Access plane: replace 'a Zotero server (reads zotero.sqlite directly, no live app needed)' with the two-channel design — zotero.org Web API for metadata + a WebDAV .prop/.zip fulltext shim on the WebDAV host; the sqlite file never reaches G11 (WebDAV sync carries attachments only, and zotero.org cannot serve WebDAV-stored files), so the recorded data path does not exist.

§Design/Access plane: replace 'an Obsidian server (needs the live desktop app)' with the in-repo filesystem kp-vault MCP over the git mirror (ADR-0029 already resolved this; the RFC body was never re-edited) — and record the corollary that Obsidian is the recommended viewer, never a dependency, per the distributability directive.

§Design/Index plane: strike 'full-text/BM25 (free via the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch)' — that implementation died with the live-app dependency; the system owns FTS via a driver interface (SQLite FTS5 embedded default; Postgres tsvector on Alex's instance). Extend the same driver-interface resolution to the graph store (Kuzu embedded default; Postgres edges cross-host), which the RFC never re-litigated after MCP moved to G11.

§Alternatives: amend 'build custom Zotero/Obsidian MCP servers from scratch — rejected; mature options exist' to: all three MCP servers are built in-repo — the named candidates either require the live desktop app, read a sqlite file that never reaches the server host, or bundle a second embedding store (54yyyu's ChromaDB) violating single-derived-index discipline; they stay thin wrappers over pyzotero/FastMCP/git.

§Design/Librarian + placement table: replace 'push-notifier digest delivery' with the ADR-0028-compliant mechanism — primary artifact is the digest note in the vault (auto-mergeable proposals/digest-* branch), plus a locally-served Atom feed Curio subscribes to (pending Curio W1), plus an optional LAN-only push-notifier 1-liner; pluggable notifier (none|file|webhook|push-notifier|command) in the OSS system; all cloud-push paths (Telegram, Pushover) are forbidden.

§Design/Ingestion plane: demote the KP-built RSS fetcher to an optional fallback for non-Curio users — the primary RSS path is consuming Curio's frontmatter-v1 exports + events-v1 stream as designated consumer #1 (a KP-side ingest adapter, never a customized Curio template); record the mandatory Curio schema-review gate (negation events, event-payload tags, ts/cursor spec) as a KP deliverable.

§Design/Librarian plane: add the disposal-side machinery the RFC omits — a separately-credentialed deterministic CI validator on the vault repo (frontmatter schema, link integrity, path allowlist, Curio-owned-file refusal) gates every proposals/* branch, the librarian's credentials can push only proposals/* refs and can never merge (GitHub Agentic Workflows 'safe outputs' doctrine); specify the interest model as derived-but-inspectable with Curio events as re-rank-only signal inside now.md scope.

§Design/Spine + Repo boundary: now.md and vault/ move OUT of the code monorepo into the canonical vault repo (hand-authored steering state is canonical content); the monorepo splits into a public product repo (GitHub-canonical, RFC-0016 Mode A mirror back to git-forge) and a private instance repo — this distributable-OSS-product scope (licensing, naming, pluggable LLM/store/notifier/harness seams, Python/uv runtime reversing the scaffold's implied Node) is a new structural fact the RFC doesn't cover and should be recorded as a formal amendment or companion RFC. ADR-0029 itself needs NO amendment: under the product/instance split every placement decision becomes instance-side configuration (one addition: a second G11 CT for the vault/retrieval MCP alongside CT 206, with G11 headroom re-derived first).
